---
title: ""
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple]
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    navigation-mode: linear
    css: presentation.css
    footer: "Congress, Cash, & Constituents"
    title-slide: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

::: {.title-slide .no-footer data-state="title"}

::: {.title-art}
<div class="title-art-stack">
  <img src="money_bag.png" class="money-bag" alt="Money bag" />
  <div class="presented-block">
```{=html}
<div class="presented">Presented by:</div>
<div class="names">Ariel Keller</div>
<div class="names">Lucille Christian</div>
```
  </div>
</div>
:::

::: {.title-text}
<h1><span class="title-main">Congress,<br>Cash, &amp;<br>Constituents:</span><br><span class="title-sub">Where the Money Goes</span></h1>
:::

:::

---

## WHAT PROBLEM DOES<br>OUR PRODUCT ADDRESS? {.problem-slide .no-footer}

- It’s hard to connect **members of Congress** to both **money in** (campaign finance) and **money out** (federal awards).
- Relevant data is **scattered across multiple systems** with inconsistent identifiers.
- We created a **single, interactive tool** that tells a clear money story for any member.

---

## KEY DESIGN DECISIONS<br>& TRADE-OFFS {.problem-slide}

- **State-first selection** to avoid ambiguous names
- **Cached member list** for speed and stability
- **Fallback committee source** (UnitedStates.io) for reliability
- **API calls on demand** to keep the tool live and up to date

---

## WHAT WE WOULD IMPROVE<br>WITH MORE TIME {.problem-slide}

- Better matching between earmarks and awards (more scoring signals)
- Smarter industry labeling (cleaner donor employer/occupation categories)
- More robust geolocation (district-level filters for USAspending)
- Add LLM summaries for plain‑language interpretation

## QUESTIONS? {.question-slide}

```{=html}
<div class="question-text">Questions?</div>
```

---

## THANK YOU {.thank-you-slide}

::: {.thank-you-wrap}
<img src="money_bag.png" class="money-bag" alt="Money bag" />
<div class="thank-you-text">THANK YOU!</div>
:::
